question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getReadmitDelay()] Can an unprivileged external user set `readmitDelay` to a very large value (e.g., 24 hours) via configuration, causing any block node that encounters a single error while serving a fee schedule update block to be marked inactive for 24 hours, effectively performing a denial-of-service on fee schedule update ingestion and freezing transaction fees at stale values?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user operating a malicious block node exploit the `pendingCount += blockItems.size()` accumulation in `BlockAssembler.append()` by sending many small `BlockItemSet` messages that each contain a single item, gradually building up `pendingCount` to just below `maxBlockItems`, and then inserting a crafted fee schedule update transaction as the last item before the limit, ensuring it is processed without triggering the block item count exception?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getResponseTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@DurationMin(millis = 100)` constraint on `responseTimeout` by setting it to exactly 100ms, then conducting a network-level attack (e.g., introducing artificial latency) against legitimate block nodes to cause their `serverStatus` responses to exceed 100ms, while a malicious block node on the same network responds within 100ms and is selected to serve blocks with modified fee schedule transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of a maximum bound on `maxBlockItems` to set it to `Integer.MAX_VALUE - 1`, causing the `pendingCount > streamProperties.getMaxBlockItems()` check to never trigger due to integer overflow when `pendingCount` wraps around, allowing a malicious block node to inject an unlimited number of fee schedule update transactions without any enforcement of the design-parameter limit?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user set `maxStreamResponseSize` to exactly 1MB (the smallest practical value), causing the `BlockNode` constructor to set `maxInboundMessageSize` to 1MB, which then causes the gRPC channel to reject any fee schedule update block that exceeds 1MB, while a malicious block node serves a crafted fee schedule update in a block smaller than 1MB that is accepted and applied?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxSubscribeAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user set `maxSubscribeAttempts` to a very large value and `readmitDelay` to a very small value (10 seconds), creating a configuration where a malicious block node is never marked inactive (due to high `maxSubscribeAttempts`) and is always readmitted quickly (due to low `readmitDelay`), allowing it to continuously serve blocks with modified fee schedule transactions without interruption?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `maxBlockItems` defaults to 800,000 but has no maximum constraint, by injecting a configuration value of `Integer.MAX_VALUE` to disable the block item count check entirely, allowing a malicious block node to send blocks with an arbitrary number of fee schedule update transactions that modify fees outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getReadmitDelay()] Can an unprivileged external user set `readmitDelay` to exactly `PT10S` (10 seconds minimum) and `maxSubscribeAttempts` to 1, creating a scenario where a malicious block node is deactivated after one error but readmitted every 10 seconds, allowing it to serve one fee-modifying block per 10-second window while legitimate nodes are bypassed due to the rapid cycling?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@DataSizeUnit(DataUnit.MEGABYTES)` annotation to inject a `maxStreamResponseSize` value of `0` megabytes (if the `@NotNull` constraint does not prevent a zero-byte DataSize), causing the gRPC channel's `maxInboundMessageSize` to be set to 0 and rejecting all block stream responses, effectively halting fee schedule update ingestion entirely?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user operating a malicious block node exploit the `BlockAssembler.onBlockItemSet()` method's lack of validation on the content of individual block items to inject a fee schedule update transaction disguised as a different block item type (e.g., `EVENT_HEADER`), bypassing any fee-specific validation and causing the mirror node to process a modified fee schedule that changes transaction fees outside of design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getResponseTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseTimeout` to a very large value (e.g., 1 hour), causing `BlockNode.getBlockRange()` to block for up to 1 hour waiting for a response from a slow or unresponsive block node, effectively stalling the fee schedule update ingestion pipeline and preventing legitimate fee schedule updates from being processed in a timely manner?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxSubscribeAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user set `maxSubscribeAttempts` to 1 and then operate a malicious block node that serves a fee-modifying block on the first subscription attempt, then deliberately fails on subsequent attempts to get marked inactive, exploiting the fact that the fee-modifying block was already processed before the node was deactivated?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Min(1000)` constraint on `maxBlockItems` by setting it to 1000 via a Spring Boot configuration override (e.g., environment variable `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_BLOCK_STREAM_MAXBLOCKITEMS=1000`), causing any fee schedule update block with more than 1000 items to be rejected, while a malicious block node serves a crafted fee schedule update in a block with exactly 999 items that is accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a maximum upper bound on `maxStreamResponseSize` to set it to a value that causes the `(int) streamProperties.getMaxStreamResponseSize().toBytes()` cast in `BlockNode` to overflow to a negative integer, causing the gRPC channel to use a negative `maxInboundMessageSize` which may be interpreted as unlimited, allowing a malicious block node to send arbitrarily large blocks with modified fee schedule transactions?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getReadmitDelay()] Can an unprivileged external user set `readmitDelay` to a very large value (e.g., `PT87600H` = 10 years), causing any block node that encounters a single error while serving a fee schedule update block to be permanently excluded from the node pool, effectively performing a permanent denial-of-service on fee schedule update ingestion if all honest nodes encounter at least one error?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxBlockItems()] Can an unprivileged external user operating a malicious block node exploit the `BlockAssembler.append()` method's check `if (firstItemCase == BLOCK_HEADER && !pending.isEmpty())` to send a block where a fee schedule update transaction is embedded between two `BLOCK_HEADER` items, causing the first block to be discarded and the fee schedule update to be processed as part of the second block without proper validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxSubscribeAttempts()] Can an unprivileged external user set `maxSubscribeAttempts` to 1 via configuration, then exploit the `errors.set(0)` reset in `onError()` (which only resets after marking the node inactive) to create a scenario where a malicious block node alternates between serving fee-modifying blocks and triggering errors, cycling through the active/inactive states to repeatedly inject fee modifications?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getMaxStreamResponseSize()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@DataSizeUnit(DataUnit.MEGABYTES)` annotation by providing a negative value for `maxStreamResponseSize` (if not caught by `@NotNull` alone), causing `DataSize.ofMegabytes()` to return a negative `DataSize`, which when converted to bytes and cast to `int` produces a negative `maxInboundMessageSize`, effectively removing the gRPC message size limit and allowing unlimited fee schedule modification data to be ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/StreamProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getResponseTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user set `responseTimeout` to exactly `PT0.1S` (100ms) and then exploit network congestion or high load on legitimate block nodes to cause their `serverStatus` responses to consistently exceed 100ms, while a malicious block node with dedicated resources always responds within 100ms, causing the mirror node to exclusively use the malicious node and process only attacker-crafted fee schedule updates?
